'''James Henry Donaldson''' (born 14 September 1991) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|lf}} and {{rlp|sr}} for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

Donaldson, a Challenge Cup winner, previously played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]] at the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. He played for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the Super League and the [[Kingstone Press Championship|Championship]], and on loan from [[Hull Kingston Rovers|Hull KR]] at the [[York City Knights]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]].

==Background==
Donaldson was born in [[Whitehaven]], [[Cumbria]], England.

Donaldson subsequently attended [[Whitehaven Academy|Whitehaven School]].

==Playing career==
He primarily plays as a {{rlp|lf}}, but he can also play in the {{rlp|sr}}.

===Early career===
He played a key role in the 2007 [[Rugby League]] European under-16's Championships, where [[Whitehaven School]] represented England.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Donaldson was much sought after as a junior and signed for the [[Bradford Bulls]], despite offers from many other top clubs.

A {{rlp|lf}}, Donaldson hails from [[Cumbria]] and he has played for the famous [[Wath Brow Hornets ARLFC|Wath Brow Hornets]] [[amateur]] side.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

===Senior career===
====Bradford Bulls====
Donaldson played for the [[Bradford Bulls]] between 2009 and 2014.
[[File:Jim Donaldson.JPG|thumb|right|James Donaldson playing for the [[Bradford Bulls]]]]

====Dewsbury Rams====
Donaldson appeared for the [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the 2013 [[rugby league]] season, as part of the [[Bradford Bulls]]' dual-registration partnership with the club.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

====Hull Kingston Rovers====
Donaldson joined [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] ahead of the 2015 [[Super League]] season.

He represented the club in the [[2015 Challenge Cup]] Final.
[[File:James Donaldson Hull KR.jpg|thumb|right|Donaldson playing for [[Hull KR]]]]

Donaldson suffered relegation from the [[Super League]] with [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the 2016 season, due to losing the [[Million Pound Game]] by the [[Salford Red Devils]].

In the [[Good Friday]] [[Championship (rugby league)|Championship]] league clash against [[Featherstone Rovers]] on 14 April 2017, Donaldson unfortunately suffered a season-ending [[anterior cruciate ligament]] (ACL) knee injury in [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' 30–22 victory at [[Craven Park, Hull|Craven Park]].

Donaldson (currently injured at the time), was part of the [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' side that won promotion back to the [[Super League]], at the first time of asking following relegation the season prior, securing automatic promotion through the 2017 [[The Qualifiers|Qualifiers]] process.<ref>{{cite news |url=https://www.zerotackle.com/james-donaldson-shuns-nrl-offers-to-remain-at-hull-kr-24257/ |title=James Donaldson 'shuns NRL offers' to remain at Hull KR |date=22 October 2016 |website=zerotackle.com |accessdate=11 June 2017 |publisher=Zero Digital Media }}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=http://www.hullkr.co.uk/on-the-pitch/news/article/donaldson-added-to-2017-squad |title=Donaldson added to 2017 squad |date=21 October 2016 |website=hullkr.co.uk |accessdate=11 June 2017 |publisher=Hull Kingston Rovers }}</ref>

Donaldson made his long-awaited return from his ACL kneeinjury on 15 March 2018, in a [[Super League]] fixture against the [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] ran-out 6–38 victors, with James featuring from off the interchange bench.

It was revealed on 10 October 2018, that Donaldson would be departing [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] following a restructure of the club's on field personnel.<ref>{{cite web|date=10 October 2018|title=Mass exodus at Hull Kingston Rovers|url=http://mobile.twitter.com/hullkrofficial/status/1050083609120567298|accessdate=10 October 2018}}</ref>

====York City Knights====
Donaldson appeared on only one occasion for the [[York City Knights]] on 11 March 2018, four-days later he made his long-awaited return to the [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]' fold, following his long-term ACL knee injury that he sustained the season prior.{{citation needed|date=October 2022}}

Donaldson scored for the [[York City Knights]] on a single dual-registration appearance, in a 22–24 victory over [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]].

====Leeds Rhinos====
It was revealed on 6 December 2018, that Donaldson had been handed a trial at the [[Leeds Rhinos]] ahead of the 2019 campaign.

Donaldson was subsequently given a chance to '''"prove his worth"''' to new [[Leeds Rhinos]] Head Coach [[David Furner]], in all hopes of earning a contract at the club on a permanent basis.

On 8 January 2019, after a successful trial, Donaldson signed a one-year contract with the [[Leeds Rhinos]].

Donaldson made his [[Leeds Rhinos]]' début on 2 February 2019, in a 26–6 round 1 [[Super League]] defeat against the [[Warrington Wolves]] at the [[Halliwell Jones Stadium]].

Donaldson scored his first try for the [[Leeds Rhinos]] on 19 April 2019, in a 38–18 victory over the [[Huddersfield Giants]].

It was announced on 12 September. Donaldson would be staying at Leeds until 2021.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2019/09/12/donaldson-agrees-new-two-year-deal-with-rhinos/|title = Donaldson agrees new two year deal with Rhinos|date = 12 September 2019}}</ref>

On 17 October 2020, he played in the [[2020 Challenge Cup Final]] victory for Leeds over Salford at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54555957|title=Leeds beat Salford 17–16 after Luke Gale lands late winning drop-goal|work=BBC Sport|access-date=15 November 2020}}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, Donaldson played for Leeds in their 24–12 loss to [[St Helens RFC]] in the [[2022 Super League Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|newspaper=The Observer|date=24 September 2022 |accessdate=29 September 2022 |last1=Bower |first1=Aaron }}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls (re-join)===
On 20 August 2024 it was reported that he would re-join [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on a 3-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=20 August 2024|accessdate=3 October 2024|author=Aaron Bower|title=James Donaldson’s next club revealed after Leeds Rhinos exit finalised|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/james-donaldsons-next-club-revealed-after-leeds-rhinos-exit-finalised}}</ref>

On 22 December 2025 he announced his retirement as a professional<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=22 Dec 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Bradford Bulls star announces sudden retirement ahead of Super League return|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/bradford-bulls-star-announces-sudden-retirement-ahead-of-super-league-return}}</ref>
